public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) EXCHANGEELEMENTKIND_INFORMATIONELEMENT_LITERAL

See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.updm.utils.UPDMProfile.EXCHANGEELEMENTKIND_INFORMATIONELEMENT_LITERAL)
GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_INSTALLATION_LITERAL
public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_INSTALLATION_LITERAL
A base, camp, post, station, yard, center, or other activity, including leased facilities, without regard to the duration of operational control. An installation may include one or more sites.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.updm.utils.UPDMProfile.GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_INSTALLATION_LITERAL)

public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_REGIONOFCOUNTRY_LITERAL
A large, usually continuous segment of a political state or nation or its territory.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.updm.utils.UPDMProfile.GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_REGIONOFCOUNTRY_LITERAL)

See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.updm.utils.UPDMProfile.GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_REGIONOFWORLD_LITERAL)

public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_COUNTRY_LITERAL

See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.updm.utils.UPDMProfile.GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_COUNTRY_LITERAL)
GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_FACILITY_LITERAL
public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_FACILITY_LITERAL
A real property entity consisting of underlying land and one or more of the following: a building, a structure (including linear structures), a utility system, or pavement.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.updm.utils.UPDMProfile.GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_FACILITY_LITERAL)
GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_GEOFEATURE_LITERAL
public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_GEOFEATURE_LITERAL
An object that encompasses meteorological, geographic, and control features mission significance.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.updm.utils.UPDMProfile.GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_GEOFEATURE_LITERAL)
GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_OTHER_LITERAL
public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_OTHER_LITERAL
Other GeoPoliticalExtent kind that is not on the enumerated list.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.updm.utils.UPDMProfile.GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_OTHER_LITERAL)
GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_SITE_LITERAL
public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_SITE_LITERAL
Physical (geographic) location that is or was owned by, leased to, or otherwise possessed. Each site is assigned to a single installation. A site may exist in one of three forms: (1) Land only, where there are no facilities present and where the land consists of either a single land parcel or two or more contiguous land parcels. (2) Facility or facilities only, where the underlying land is neither owned nor controlled by the government. A stand-alone facility can be a site. If a facility is not a stand-alone facility, it must be assigned to a site. (3). Land and all the facilities thereon, where the land consists of either a single land parcel or two or more contiguous land parcels.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.updm.utils.UPDMProfile.GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_SITE_LITERAL)
GEOPOLITICALEXTENTTYPEKIND_OTHERTYPE_LITERAL

public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) INFORMATIONKIND_DATA_LITERAL

ROLEKIND_EQUIPMENT_LITERAL

 MODAF: (MODAF::PhysicalAsset): Usage of an ResourceArtifact (MODAF::Artefact) as a component of a ResourceConfiguration.
 DoDAF: NA

 MODAF: Usage of an Artefact (UPDM::ResourceArtifact) as a part of another Artefact (UPDM::ResourceArtifact), equates to a MODAF::Part

ARCHITECTURALDESCRIPTION_PURPOSE_PROPERTY

Deprecated.
use ArchitecturalDescriptionStereotype.PURPOSE
Explains the need for the Architecture, what it will demonstrate, the types of analyses that will be applied to it, who is expected to perform the analyses, what decisions are expected to be made on the basis of each form of analysis, who is expected to make those decisions, and what actions are expected to result.

Deprecated.
use ArchitecturalDescriptionStereotype.RECOMMENDATIONS
States the recommendations that have been developed based on the architecture effort. Examples include recommended system implementations, and opportunities for technology insertion.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.updm.utils.UPDMProfile.ARCHITECTURALDESCRIPTION_RECOMMENDATIONS_PROPERTY)

[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public [Stereotype](../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html) getActivity()
Deprecated.
use getInstance(element).activity().getStereotype()
UPDM: An abstract element that represents a behavior (i.e. a Function or OperationalActivity) that can be performed by a Performer.
 MODAF: NA
 DoDAF: Work, not specific to a single organization, weapon system or individual that transforms inputs (Resources) into outputs (Resources) or changes their state.
Returns:
stereotype

getActualLocation
[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public [Stereotype](../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html) getActualLocation()
Deprecated.
use getInstance(element).actualLocation().getStereotype()
MODAF: A PhysicalLocation (MODAF::ActualLocation) is a location anywhere on the earth. The means of describing the location is a string (locationDescription). The information contained in that string is governed by the taxonomy reference - e.g. if the PhysicalLocation is a GPS reference , the string will contain the GPS coordinates. NOTE: this has been extended in UPDM to include non-earth locations.
 DoDAF: All subtypes of << IndividualType>> Location, such as Facility, Site, etc.
Returns:
stereotype
isActualLocation

getContract
[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public [Stereotype](../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html) getContract()
Deprecated.
use getInstance(element).contract().getStereotype()
A specialization of an OperationalExchange a Contract specifies an agreement between two or more parties to exchange information. The Contract forms an ontological commitment between parties in a community of interest (CoI) or Community of Practice (CoP). The contract is also used to realize the information exchange requirements of either a needline or a community of interest.
Returns:
stereotype

 MODAF:A rule governing an operational behaviour or property.
 DoDAF:A principle or condition that governs behavior; a prescribed guide for conduct or action (Rule).

MODAF: An OperationalEventTrace (MODAF::OperationalInteractionSpecification) is a specification of the interactions between nodes in an operational architecture.
 DoDAF: The Operational Event-Trace Description (OV-6c) DoDAF-described View provides a time ordered examination of the resource flows as a result of a particular scenario. Each event-trace diagram will have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.
Returns:
stereotype

getOperationalExchange
[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public [Stereotype](../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html) getOperationalExchange()
Deprecated.
use getInstance(element).operationalExchange().getStereotype()
UPDM: An utility element used as common flow for:
 - InformationExchange
 - OrganizationalExchange
 - EnergyExchange
 - MaterielExchange
 - ConfigurationExchange
 - GeoPoliticalExtent
 An operational exchange is formed when an activity of one operational node consumes items produced by another activity of a different operational node.
 An operational exchange describes the characteristics of the exchanged item, such as the content, format (voice, imagery, text and message format, etc.), throughput requirements, security or classification level, timeliness requirement, and the degree of interoperability.

 MODAF: An OperationalExchange (MODAF::LogicalFlow) asserts that a flow exists or is required between Nodes (e.g. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).
Returns:
stereotype

UPDM: A state machine describing an operational behavior or property.
 MODAF: An OperationalStateMachine (MODAF::OperationalStateDescription) is a rule governing an operational behaviour or property.
 DoDAF: The Operational State Transition Description (OV-6b) DoDAF-described View is a graphical method of describing how an Operational Activity responds to various events by changing its state. The diagram represents the sets of events to which the Architecture will respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

stereotype

[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public [Stereotype](../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html) getRequest()
Deprecated.
use getInstance(element).request().getStereotype()
UPDM:From SOAML A Request represents a feature of a Participant that is the consumption of a service by one
 participant provided by others using well-defined terms, conditions and interfaces. A
 Request designates ports that define the connection point through which a Participant
 meets its needs through the consumption of services provided by others.
 MODAF:Simil to requires, Asserts that a Resource requires a Service to be provided in order to function correctly.
 DoDAF: Similar to ServicePort, A part of a Performer that specifics a distinct interaction point through which the Performer interacts with other Performers. This isolates dependencies between performers to particular interaction points rather than to the performer as a whole.
Returns:
stereotype

UPDM: A contractual agreement between two resources that implement protocols through which the source service interacts to the destination resource.
 A physical connection between two resources that implements protocols through which the source resource can transmit items to the destination resource.
 MODAF: The mechanism by which a Service communicates.
 DoDAF: An overlap between Performers for the purpose of producing a Resource that is
 consumed by the other. (DoDAF::Interface).
 SOAML: Defines the interface to a Service Point or Request Point and is the type of a role in a service contract.
Returns:
stereotype

The extendedStereotype tagged value shall contain the name of the meta-model stereotype which is extended.  DoDAF: NA

getTemporalPart
[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public [Stereotype](../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html) getTemporalPart()
Deprecated.
use getInstance(element).temporalPart().getStereotype()
UPDM Artifact: An EnterprisePhase can be sub-divided into structural and temporal parts. TemporalPart describes the EnterprisePhase elements that have a time based nature.
 MODAF: Asserts that one EnterprisePhase is a temporal part of another. Note: This means that both EnterprisePhases have the same spatial extent - i..e this is only a temporal structure (MODAF:: EnterpriseTemporalPart).
Returns:
stereotype

Deprecated.
use getInstance(element).transactional().getStereotype()
A specialization of InformationElement the enables the specification of reusable information building blocks, upon which multiple community semantics can be built. Transactionals describe the constructions plans for data sets realizable from the underlying information/data store. The transactional links the community semantics to the structures and business rules information/data store.
Returns:
stereotype

<div class="col-last odd-row-color">
<div class="block">A real property entity consisting of underlying land and one or more of the following: a building, a structure (including linear structures), a utility system, or pavement.</div>
</div>

</div>

<li>
<section class="detail" id="GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_SITE_LITERAL">
<h3>GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_SITE_LITERAL</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="modifiers">public static final</span> <span class="return-type"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">String</a></span> <span class="element-name">GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_SITE_LITERAL</span></div>
<div class="block">Physical (geographic) location that is or was owned by, leased to, or otherwise possessed. Each site is assigned to a single installation. A site may exist in one of three forms: (1) Land only, where there are no facilities present and where the land consists of either a single land parcel or two or more contiguous land parcels. (2) Facility or facilities only, where the underlying land is neither owned nor controlled by the government. A stand-alone facility can be a site. If a facility is not a stand-alone facility, it must be assigned to a site. (3). Land and all the facilities thereon, where the land consists of either a single land parcel or two or more contiguous land parcels.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>See Also:</dt>
<dd>
<ul class="see-list">
<li><a href="../../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.updm.utils.UPDMProfile.GEOPOLITICALEXTENTKIND_SITE_LITERAL">Constant Field Values</a></li>
</ul>
</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<section class="detail" id="getActivity()">
<h3>getActivity</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getActivity</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).activity().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">UPDM:  An abstract element that represents a behavior (i.e. a Function or OperationalActivity) that can be performed by a Performer.
         MODAF: NA
         DoDAF: Work, not specific to a single organization, weapon system or individual that transforms inputs (Resources) into outputs (Resources) or changes their state.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>
<section class="detail" id="isActivity(com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element)">

<section class="detail" id="getActualLocation()">
<h3>getActualLocation</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getActualLocation</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).actualLocation().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">MODAF: A PhysicalLocation (MODAF::ActualLocation) is a location anywhere on the earth. The means of describing the location is a string (locationDescription). The information contained in that string is governed by the taxonomy reference - e.g. if the PhysicalLocation is a    GPS reference   , the string will contain the GPS coordinates.  NOTE: this has been extended in UPDM to include non-earth locations.
         DoDAF: All subtypes of &lt;&lt; IndividualType&gt;&gt; Location, such as Facility, Site, etc.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>

<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>

<div class="block">MODAF: A composite structure representing the physical and human resources (and their interactions) in an enterprise.--A CapabilityConfiguration is a set of artefacts or an organisation configured to provide a capability, and should be guided by [doctrine] which may take the form of Standard or OperationalConstraint stereotypes.
         DoDAF: Any entity - human, automated, or any aggregation of human and/or automated - that performs an activity and provides a capability (Performer).</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).condition().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">MODAF: A definition of the conditions in which something exists or functions.  An Environment may be specified in terms of LocationType (e.g. terrain), Climate (e.g. tropical), and LightCondition (e.g. dark, light, dusk, etc.)
         DoDAF: An object that encompasses meteorological, geographic, and control features mission significance.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<section class="detail" id="getContract()">
<h3>getContract</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>

<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).contract().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">A specialization of an    OperationalExchange    a    Contract    specifies an agreement between two or more parties to exchange information.  The Contract forms an ontological commitment between parties  in a community of interest (CoI) or Community of Practice (CoP).   The contract is also used to realize the information exchange requirements of either a needline or a community of interest.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).designRule().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">A design rule is a solution to a problem in a specific context with the following characteristics:
         -belongs to a problem domain,
         -packages knowledge in a reusable form,
         -standardize solutions to design problems within NBD,
         -gives value to the re-user.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<div class="block">UPDM: An extension of &lt;<activityedge>&gt; that is used to model the flow of control/objects through a Function.

<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>

<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).locationType().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">MODAF: A general specification of the surroundings / scenario in which an operation may take place. Examples would be: "desert", "arctic", "at sea", etc.
         DoDAF: A point or extent in space that may be referred to physically or logically. Includes concepts such as: Facility, Installation, RealProperty, Site, , and instances of conditions such as underwater (as specified in UJTLs).</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).logicalDataModel().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">MODAF: A LogicalDataModel is a specification of business information requirements as a formal data structure, where relationships and classes (entities) are used to specify the logic which underpins the information.
         DoDAF: A Logical Data Model allows analysis of an architecture   s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation specific or product specific issues.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>

<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).measurement().getStereotype()</div>

<div class="block">MODAF: MeasurableProperty: A property of something in the physical world, expressed in amounts of a unit of measure. The property may have a required value - either specified by the [defaultValue] from UML::property attribute, or the [minValue] and [maxValue] to specify a required range.
         DoDAF: Measure: A Measurement (DoDAF::Measure) is the magnitude of some attribute of an individual.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

</dl>

<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).operationalActivity().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">MODAF: A logical process, specified independently of how the process is carried out.DoDAF:  An activity is an action performed in conducting the business of an enterprise. It is a general term that does not imply a placement in a hierarchy
         (e.g., it could be a process or a task as defined in other documents and it could be at any level of the hierarchy of the OV-5). It is used to portray operational actions not hardware/software system functions.  NOTE: This is also a specialization of Activity.
         DoDAF:NA</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<div class="block">MODAF: An OperationalEventTrace (MODAF::OperationalInteractionSpecification) is a specification of the interactions between nodes in an operational architecture.
         DoDAF: The Operational Event-Trace Description (OV-6c) DoDAF-described View provides a time ordered examination of the resource flows as a result of a particular scenario. Each event-trace diagram will have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<section class="detail" id="getOperationalExchange()">
<h3>getOperationalExchange</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getOperationalExchange</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).operationalExchange().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">UPDM: An utility element used as common flow for:
         - InformationExchange
         - OrganizationalExchange
         - EnergyExchange
         - MaterielExchange
         - ConfigurationExchange
         - GeoPoliticalExtent
         An operational exchange is formed when an activity of one operational node consumes items produced by another activity of a different operational node.
         An operational exchange describes the characteristics of the exchanged item, such as the content, format (voice, imagery, text and message format, etc.), throughput requirements, security or classification level, timeliness requirement, and the degree of interoperability.

         MODAF: An OperationalExchange (MODAF::LogicalFlow) asserts that a flow exists or is required between Nodes (e.g. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>

<section class="detail" id="getOperationalStateDescription()">
<h3>getOperationalStateDescription</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getOperationalStateDescription</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).operationalStateDescription().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">UPDM: A state machine describing an operational behavior or property.
         MODAF: An OperationalStateMachine (MODAF::OperationalStateDescription) is a rule governing an operational behaviour or property.
         DoDAF: The Operational State Transition Description (OV-6b) DoDAF-described View is a graphical method of describing how an Operational Activity responds to various events by changing its state. The diagram represents the sets of events to which the Architecture will respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>

<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).physicalDataModel().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">MODAF:  A PhysicalDataModel is an implementable specification of a data structure. A PhysicalDataModel realises a LogicalDataModel, taking into account implementation restrictions and performance issues whilst still enforcing the constraints, relationships and typing of the logical model.
         DoDAF: A Physical Data Model defines the structure of the various kinds of system or service data that are utilized by the systems or services in the Architecture.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<dd>stereotype</dd>

<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>

<li>
<section class="detail" id="getRequest()">
<h3>getRequest</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getRequest</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).request().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">UPDM:From SOAML A Request represents a feature of a Participant that is the consumption of a service by one
         participant provided by others using well-defined terms, conditions and interfaces. A
         Request designates ports that define the connection point through which a Participant
         meets its needs through the consumption of services provided by others.
         MODAF:Simil to requires, Asserts that a Resource requires a Service to be provided in order to function correctly.
         DoDAF: Similar to ServicePort, A part of a Performer that specifics a distinct interaction point through which the Performer interacts with other Performers. This isolates dependencies between performers to particular interaction points rather than to the performer as a whole.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>

</div>
<div class="block">UPDM: ResourceInterface is a contractual agreement between two resources that implement protocols through which the source resource to the destination resource.
         MODAF: NA
         DoDAF: An overlap between Performers for the purpose of producing a Resource that is consumed by the other (DoDAF:: Interface).</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).resourceMessage().getStereotype()</div>

<div class="block">UPDM: Message for use in a Resource Event-Trace, implements a ResourceInteraction.
         MODAF: A specification of the interactions between aspects of a Resources architecture (MODAF::ResourceInteractionSpecification).
         DoDAF:  An overlap of an Activity with a Resource, in particular a consuming or producing Activity that expresses an input, output, consumption, or production Activity of the Resource (DoDAF::  activityResourceOverlap).</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>

<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>

</div>

         DoDAF:The group of Information Security Marking attributes in which the use of attributes 'classification' and 'ownerProducer' is required. This group is to be contrasted with group 'SecurityAttributesOptionGroup' in which use of those attributes is optional.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

</dl>

<div class="block">MODAF:A type of delivered functionality, specified independently of the resources that provide it.
         DoDAF: mechanism to enable access to a set of one or more capabilities , where the access is provided using a prescribed interface and is exercised consistent with constraints and policies as specified by the service description.  The mechanism is a Performer.  The "capabilities" accessed are Resources -- Information, Data, Materiel, Performers, and Geo-political Extents.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<div class="block">UPDM:  A ServiceFunction describes the abstract behavior of ServiceOperations, regardless of the actual implementation.
         MODAF: A type of activity describing the functionality of a service.
         DoDAF: Information necessary to interact with the service in such terms as the service inputs, outputs, and associated semantics. The service description also conveys what is accomplished when the service is invoked and the conditions for using the service.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getServiceInterface</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).serviceInterface().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">UPDM: A contractual agreement between two resources that implement protocols through which the source service interacts to the destination resource.
         A physical connection between two resources that implements protocols through which the source resource can transmit items to the destination resource.
         MODAF: The mechanism by which a Service communicates.
         DoDAF: An overlap between Performers for the purpose of producing a Resource that is
         consumed by the other. (DoDAF::Interface).
         SOAML: Defines the interface to a Service Point or Request Point and is the type of a role in a service contract.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

</div>
<div class="block">UPDM: A ServiceOperation provides the access point for invoking the behavior of a provided service.  The ServiceOperations are defined on ServiceInterfaces and mirrored on the providing Resource to handle calls forwarded on by the interface.
         MODAF: a function or procedure which enables programmatic communication with a Service via a ServiceInterface (MODAF:: ServiceInterfaceOpration).</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

</div>
<div class="block">MODAF: An executable computer programme.
         DoDAF: Materiel: Equipment, apparatus or supplies that are of interest, without distinction as to its application for administrative or combat purposes.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>

</dl>
</section>

         DoDAF: Work, not specific to a single organization, weapon system or individual, that transforms inputs into
         outputs or changes their state (DoDAF:: Activity).</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).stereotypeExtension().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">MODAF: Defines an additional stereotype used in the architecture which is not defined in this meta-model. The body attribute contains the name of the new stereotype. The extendedStereotype tagged value shall contain the name of the meta-model stereotype which is extended. The ontologyReference tagged value shall be populated with a reference to the external ontology element represented by the new stereotype.
         DoDAF: NA</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>

<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).structuralPart().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">UPDM: An EnterprisePhase can be sub-divided into structural and temporal parts. StructuralPart describes the EnterprisePhase elements that describe the structure.
         MODAF: Asserts that one EnterprisePhase is a spatial part of another, (MODAF::EnterpriseStructure) Note:- This is a topological structuring relationship, hence the EnterprisePhase may be physically disjoint</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<section class="detail" id="getTechnicalStandard()">
<h3>getTechnicalStandard</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>

<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).technicalStandard().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">MODAF:A ratified and peer-reviewed specification that is used to guide or constrain the architecture. A Standard may be applied to any element in the architecture via the [constrainedItem] property of UML::Constraint (Standard).
         DoDAF:Technical standards document specific technical methodologies and practices to design and implement.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<section class="detail" id="getTemporalPart()">
<h3>getTemporalPart</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getTemporalPart</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).temporalPart().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">UPDM Artifact: An EnterprisePhase can be sub-divided into structural and temporal parts. TemporalPart describes the EnterprisePhase elements that have a time based nature.
         MODAF: Asserts that one EnterprisePhase is a temporal part of another. Note: This means that both EnterprisePhases have the same spatial extent - i..e this is only a temporal structure (MODAF:: EnterpriseTemporalPart).</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<section class="detail" id="getTransactional()">
<h3>getTransactional</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getTransactional</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).transactional().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">A specialization of    InformationElement    the enables the specification of reusable information building blocks, upon which multiple community semantics can be built.  Transactionals describe the constructions plans for data sets realizable from the underlying information/data store.  The transactional links the community semantics to the structures and business rules information/data store.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).trustline().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">MODAF:Asserts that the trustingParty (either a Node or a KnownResource) trusts the trustedParty to a given level (indicated by the level attribute). Note:  No unit of measure is associated with the level - security architects muist define their own scale of trust levels for a given architecture or set of architectures.
         DoDAF:NA</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
